folio.label: 141v
folio.l: And witú that word , she fel a swougúne a non
And after , whan hire swougúnyngö is a gon
She risetú vp , and to hire fader seide
Blissed be god , that I shal dye a mayde
ñif me my detú , er that I haue a shame
Dotú witú ©oure child , ©oure wil , a goddes name
And witú that word , she preietú hi~ ful ofte
That witú his swerd , he shulde smyte softe
And witú that word , a swougúne doun she fil
Hire fader , witú ful sorweful herte , and wil
Hir hed of smot , and by the top it hente
And to the Iuge , he gan it to päsente
As he sat ©et , in doom Consistorie
And whan the Iuge it saw , as seitú the storie
He bad to take hi~ , and an hange hi~ faste
But rigút a noon , a thousand peeple in thraste
To saue the knygút , for routhe and for pitee
For knowen was , this fals Iniquitee
¢ The peeple a noon , hadden suspect in this thyngö
By manere of the cherles , chalangyngö
That it was by the assent , of Apius
They wisten wel , that he was lecherous
For whicú , vn to this Apius they gon
And cast hi~ in a prisou~ , rigút a noon
Where as he slow hi~ self , and Claudyus
That seruau~t was , vn to this Apius
Was demed for to hange , vp on a tree
But that Virginius , of his pitee
So preyed for hi~ , that he was exiled
And elles certes , had he ben bigyled
The remenau~t were an hanged , moore and lesse
That were consentant , of this cursednesse
¢ Heere moun men sen , how synne hatú his merite
Betú war , for noman woot , whom god wol smyte
In no degree , ne in whicú manere wyse
The worm of conscience , may a gryse
Of wykked lyf , thougú it so pryue be
That no man wot ther of , but god and he
For be he lewed man , or elles lered
He noot how sone , that he shal ben a fered
Therfore I rede ©ow , this counseil take
Forsaketú synne , er synne ©ow forsake

note.notetxt: 284There are two crosses in dry point at the end of the folio. One perhaps refering to the rubric. Lighter ink than the one for the text: written at a different stage? Double check .ODR

folio.l: ¢ Here endetú the Phisiciens tale ,
